cb_step_1.title: Select the Pelvic CT Scan
cb_step_1.simple: Choose the Pelvic Volume from the input list.
cb_step_1.detailed: This step provides the CT scan of the pelvis that will be used for segmentation and planning. Select the appropriate volume from the list to proceed.

cb_step_2.title: Segment the Pelvis
cb_step_2.simple: Click the 'Run Step 1: Segment Pelvis' button.
cb_step_2.detailed: The tool will automatically identify and segment the pelvic bone from the CT scan, creating a 3D model needed for fracture analysis and surgical planning. Click the button to start the segmentation.

cb_step_3.title: Segment Fracture Lines
cb_step_3.simple: Click the 'Run Step 2: Segment Fractures' button.
cb_step_3.detailed: Automatically detect and segment the fracture lines of the pelvis. This step separates the bone into individual fracture fragments that will be used for reduction planning.

cb_step_4.title: Exclude Unstable or Irrelevant Fragments
cb_step_4.simple: Untick any fracture segments you wish to exclude from the planning.
cb_step_4.detailed: You may choose to exclude small or non-critical fragments from the reduction plan to simplify the workflow. Untick the boxes next to segments that should not be included in the reduction or screw planning.

cb_step_5.title: Generate a Reduction Template
cb_step_5.simple: Click the 'Run Step 3: Generate Template' button.
cb_step_5.detailed: The tool will generate an anatomical template based on the intact pelvis or a statistical shape model. This template serves as the target shape for fracture reduction.

cb_step_6.title: Register and Reduce Fractures
cb_step_6.simple: Click the 'Run Step 4: Register & Reduce' button.
cb_step_6.detailed: Automatically align the fracture fragments to the template, simulating the reduction of the pelvis to its anatomical position. This step computes the optimal position for each fragment.

cb_step_7.title: Decide on Manual Fragment Adjustment
cb_step_7.simple: Choose whether to manually adjust a fragment or skip to screw planning.
cb_step_7.detailed: If the automatic reduction is satisfactory, choose 'No' to proceed directly to screw planning. Otherwise, select 'Yes' to fine-tune the alignment of a specific fragment.

cb_step_8.title: Select a Fragment to Adjust
cb_step_8.simple: Choose which fragment needs adjustment from the dropdown list.
cb_step_8.detailed: Pick the fragment you wish to manually reposition or rotate. This allows precise correction of any residual displacement not captured by the automatic reduction.

cb_step_9.title: Adjust Fragment Position and Rotation
cb_step_9.simple: Manually move and rotate the selected fragment to refine its alignment.
cb_step_9.detailed: Fine-tune the fragment’s position and orientation for optimal anatomical reduction. Use the 3D viewer to achieve the best fit against surrounding bone.

cb_step_10.title: Apply Your Manual Adjustments
cb_step_10.simple: Click the 'Apply adjustments' button to finalize the changes.
cb_step_10.detailed: Confirm your manual repositioning of the fragment. The tool will update the reduction model with your adjustments.

cb_step_11.title: Plan Screw Placement
cb_step_11.simple: Click the 'Run Step 5: Plan Screws' button.
cb_step_11.detailed: Automatically generate screw trajectories for fixation based on the reduced pelvis. This step provides a starting point for safe screw placement.

cb_step_12.title: Decide on Screw Trajectory Editing
cb_step_12.simple: Choose whether to edit the screw trajectories or stop here.
cb_step_12.detailed: If the planned screws are acceptable, select 'No' to complete the workflow. If adjustments are needed, select 'Yes' to modify the trajectories.

cb_step_13.title: Adjust Screw Trajectories
cb_step_13.simple: Manually adjust the position and rotation of the screw trajectory lines.
cb_step_13.detailed: Refine the planned screw paths to avoid critical structures such as nerves or vessels. Use the interactive lines to position each screw optimally.

cb_step_14.title: Regenerate Screws from Edited Lines
cb_step_14.simple: Click the 'Regenerate screw from edited lines' button.
cb_step_14.detailed: Update the 3D screw models based on your manual trajectory adjustments. This finalizes the modified screw plan.

cb_step_15.title: Finish Editing Screw Trajectories
cb_step_15.simple: Untick the 'Edit Screw trajectories' checkbox to return to the main workflow.
cb_step_15.detailed: Conclude the screw trajectory editing session. The plan is now complete and ready for surgical use.
